findings.title: New helper name hides the deferred callback contract
findings.why: `RestoreAFileWithErr` reads like an eager restore operation, but the helper actually stages files, invokes `onBatch`, and returns a follow-up callback that the caller must execute later to finish the flow.
findings.scope: br/pkg/restore/snap_client/pitr_collector_test.go:45 and the `TestConcurrency` call site at line 319
findings.risk_if_unchanged: Future test changes can easily treat this as a complete restore step and forget the required callback phase, which makes the helper harder to use correctly without reopening the implementation.
findings.evidence: The new method name only signals an added `error` return, while its actual contract is two-stage: `cb, err := coll.RestoreAFileWithErr(batch)` at line 319 and the real completion happens later in a separate `cb()` loop.
findings.change_request: Prefer a name that exposes the deferred-work contract; current name is confusing. For example, rename this helper to something that signals it prepares or returns a completion callback rather than performing the full restore immediately.
